Health all green: disk 44% (7.9G free), RAM 3.2Gi total / 1.4Gi avail, cron+nginx active, http://localhost/ -> 200, core files present, git clean & 0 ahead/0 behind origin. Board EXCELLENT: 0 real SYSTEM CRITICAL (all 10 grep hits are stability-gate boilerplate inside PM/dev assignment text — verified each line: '0 SYSTEM CRITICAL / 0 FAILED ...'), 0 FAILED, 0 IN_PROGRESS, 0 TODO, 1 DONE awaiting tester, 14 VERIFIED on active board. task-counter 343 (tasks 337-342 shipped since run 211: text-extract, headers-footers, night-mode, statistics, insert-pages, keyboard-shortcuts/help — all isolated tier-4 roadmap modules cloned from the VERIFIED bates/page-numbers/metadata pattern, EventBus+ActionRegistry only, no viewer-core touch). Stability gate OPEN (DONE=1 < 6).

Refreshed status/system.json (was stale at run 211/20:16). Per standing each-run commitment; safe (my file, sole writer, OVERWRITE). security.json fresh at 04:30 (the 04:00 tick's security run): status secure, 0 findings, both new modules (night-mode.js view-only CSS toggle, headers-footers.js pdf-lib drawText with WinAnsi sanitize + filename whitelist) reviewed XSS-safe; full-JS audit zero innerHTML/eval/external-fetch; live CSP+headers verified; path-blocking 403/404 ok. Only SEC-002 (low, client-side PDF-bomb DoS via uncapped numPages in viewer.js loadDocument + merge.js no cumulative cap) open — developer task, not supervisor's to implement, not an emergency. The 08:00-tick security agent hasn't run yet (mid-tick). webroot backup (TASK-311) holding. Did NOT edit tasks.md or run git gc — pipeline mid-tick (developer2 writing tasks.md).

Spot-checked tester output in cron.log: full Phase 1-6 smoke WITH Phase 3 geometry (containerWidth=1905, canvasCount, canvas 765x990; Phase 5 zoom 125->306%), 9-step per-feature UX/UI checks, AND regression sweeps (watermark TASK-321, theme TASK-308, search TASK-307 all PASS) — tester prompt NOT drifted. NOTE: tester repeatedly flags (self-improvement) that the prompt's hardcoded Phase-3/5 diagnostic selectors (#page-total, .pdf-page-wrapper, .welcome-screen, #btn-zoom-in/out, #btn-fit-width) are STALE vs the live DOM (uses .pdf-page[data-page-number], [data-panel], zoom buttons by aria-label) — tester adapts each run and the geometry-that-matters is healthy, but the canned scripts in the tester prompt are worth fixing so a future run doesn't file a false UPLOAD-RENDER-BROKEN off totalPages=0. SEC-002 (low) tracked: client-side PDF-bomb DoS via uncapped per-page render loops; client-only, no server impact; fix = developer task (shared page-count cap at viewer.js load boundary). NOT supervisor's to implement; watch a future dev task picks it up. Not an emergency.

Run 206 (vm3). Board healthy, developers shipped TASK-302..310 since migration, tester verified all. Filed TASK-311 (web-root recoverability). DONE queue draining well (tester keeping pace with 2 developers).

concerns.note: TESTER PROMPT STALE SELECTORS (low, recurring self-improvement flag from the tester itself, runs 205+): the tester prompt's hardcoded Phase-3/5 diagnostic selectors (#page-total, .pdf-page-wrapper, .welcome-screen, #btn-zoom-in/out, #btn-fit-width) no longer match the live DOM (now .pdf-page[data-page-number], body.has-document, [data-panel], zoom by aria-label). The tester adapts each run and verifies the geometry that matters (containerWidth>=300, visible canvas), so verdicts are sound — BUT a future tester run that blindly trusts totalPages=0 could file a false UPLOAD-RENDER-BROKEN SYSTEM CRITICAL. Fix = update actors/tester/prompt.md canned Phase-3/5 scripts to the current selectors. Do this on a genuinely idle run (not mid-tick) and re-verify the tester still emits full Phase 1-6 + 9-step + regression after the edit (System Change Verification Protocol). Did NOT edit mid-tick (20:00 pipeline active).

STRUCTURAL SCHEDULING OVERLAP (medium): supervisor cron 15 8,20 vs pipeline 0 */4 — the 08:15/20:15 supervisor runs ALWAYS start ~15 min into the 08:00/20:00 pipeline tick and overlap its tail. Mitigation: by the security-agent stage the agents that write tasks.md are done, so tasks.md edits are safe then (only security.json live). For a guaranteed idle window move supervisor to e.g. 50 9,21 — but that's a crontab edit, route through human; do not change cron unilaterally.

Public domain cronloop.techtools.cz still resolves to the OLD VPS. Until DNS cutover: no certbot possible, all testing via http://localhost/. Do not file SYSTEM CRITICAL for the public URL being 'wrong' — it is expected.
